fieldIssues.evidenceQuote: Clifford Laconia Jordan (September 2, 1931 – March 27, 1993) was an American jazz tenor saxophone player and composer.
Clifford Laconia Jordan (September 2, 1931 – March 27, 1993) was an American jazz tenor saxophone player and composer.
was an American jazz tenor saxophone player and composer

notes: Entity match solid (opensearch top hit, American jazz saxophonist 1931-1993). Bio is a one-line generic template with no entity-specific prose, so no entity-collision signal is even possible; its only factual content, the Hard Bop association, is supported by the evidence ('playing hard bop with a group that included Norman Simmons, Victor Sproles, and Cy Touff'). Verdict driven by fields: birthYear 0 as shipped contradicts the documented 1931 (spec lists wrong birth year under factual_error). birthYear 0 / deathYear null / instruments [] read as missing-data placeholders, not collision artifacts; deathYear and instruments entries are missing-not-wrong, included with fill values for the repair-writer.

fieldIssues.evidenceQuote: The Jazz Composer's Orchestra was an American jazz group, founded by Carla Bley and Michael Mantler in 1965, to perform orchestral avant-garde jazz.

notes: Entity match solid (the Bley/Mantler ensemble; our name matches exactly). Template-stub bio; calling it 'one of the defining figures of Free Jazz' is editorial framing whose genre tag the evidence neither states nor contradicts — evidence says 'orchestral avant-garde jazz' with free-jazz-circle soloists (Cecil Taylor, Don Cherry, Pharoah Sanders), an allied but not identical label; per absence-is-not-contradiction this stays a note, not a wrongClaim. For a group, birthYear presumably means founding year; shipped 0 contradicts the documented 1965 founding. deathYear null: evidence gives 'The group's last performance was in 1975' if the schema wants an end year for groups. instruments [] is arguably correct for an orchestra, so not listed as an issue.

fieldIssues.evidenceQuote: Ernest Anthony Puente Jr. (April 20, 1923 – May 31, 2000), commonly known as Tito Puente, was an American musician, songwriter, bandleader, timbalero, vibraphonist and record producer.
Ernest Anthony Puente Jr. (April 20, 1923 – May 31, 2000), commonly known as Tito Puente, was an American musician, songwriter, bandleader, timbalero, vibraphonist and record producer.
was an American musician, songwriter, bandleader, timbalero, vibraphonist and record producer

wrongClaims.ourClaim: one of the defining figures of Swing
wrongClaims.problem: Evidence positively characterizes Puente's idiom as mambo and Latin jazz, not swing; the page's short description is 'American Latin jazz and mambo musician (1923–2000)'.
wrongClaims.evidenceQuote: He composed dance-oriented mambo and Latin jazz music.

notes: Entity match solid — this is the right Tito Puente, so not an entity_mismatch; but the template bio's one substantive tag (Swing) conflicts with the evidence's positive genre characterization (mambo/Latin jazz). Possible benign explanation: the site's era taxonomy may bucket him by period (1940s big-band/Machito era — evidence notes he replaced the drummer in Machito's band) rather than idiom; still misleading as shipped. Fields are missing-data placeholders; birthYear 0 contradicts documented 1923. deathYear/instruments entries are missing-not-wrong, included as fill values.

Bebop tag in our template bio is supported by it. Verdict driven by birthYear 0 vs documented 1925; deathYear/instruments are missing-not-wrong placeholders with fill values. Opensearch returned no page and zero candidates — no evidence document at all, nothing checkable. Ours is the same one-line generic template (Cool Jazz tag) with placeholder fields (birthYear 0, deathYear null, instruments []), so there is also no entity-specific prose to mismatch. The name is highly generic; any future title-search resolution carries exactly the disambiguation-collision risk this audit exists for — evidence-gathering for this record should anchor on its recordings/associated artists (e.g., via MusicBrainz credits) rather than a bare title search. The Cool Jazz tag is also the kind of value worth human review once evidence exists, since a Latin jazz group filed under Cool Jazz is at least surprising on its face.

fieldIssues.evidenceQuote: Mamie Smith (née Robinson; May 26, 1891 – August or September 16, 1946) was an American singer.

was an American singer. As a vaudeville singer, she performed in multiple styles, including jazz and blues.

notes: Entity match solid (American vaudeville singer 1891-1946). Template-stub bio; the Early Jazz tag is supported in period and substance — 'she performed in multiple styles, including jazz and blues', toured with 'Mamie Smith & Her Jazz Hounds' (lineups included Coleman Hawkins and Bubber Miley), recordings from 1920 — though the evidence frames her primarily as a blues pioneer ('the first African-American artist to make vocal blues recordings'), worth reflecting in any rewritten bio. Verdict driven by birthYear 0 vs documented 1891. Repair caution on birth year: older sources said 1883 (her first headstone even read 1883-1946) but the evidence states a 2018-discovered birth certificate gives 1891 — use 1891. deathYear/instruments are missing-not-wrong placeholders with fill values.
